title: galium uliginosum var. genuinum
intro: Galium uliginosum var. genuinum, also known as the bog aspen or marsh bedstraw, is a flowering plant species within the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its slender stems and small white flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Galium uliginosum var. genuinum are said to include reducing fever, treating wounds, and soothing digestive issues. Galium uliginosum var. genuinum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which is often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
